label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so the question is whether, following these steps, I return to the starting point. The instructions are to take 3 steps, then 10 steps, then 4 steps, then 1 step. The options are yes or no. But there's a problem here: the instructions don't mention the direction of the steps. If I don't know in which direction each set of steps is taken, I can't determine whether I end up back at the starting point. For example, if all steps are in the same direction, then I would end up 3 + 10 + 4 + 1 = 18 steps away from the starting point, so I wouldn't return. Or if the directions alternate (e.g., 3 steps forward, 10 steps back, 4 steps forward, 1 step back), then the net displacement would be 3 - 10 + 4 - 1 = -4 steps, so I would be 4 steps behind the starting point. If the directions are in a closed loop (like a square or triangle), the total displacement might be zero and I would return to the starting point. But since no directions are given, I cannot determine whether I return
